$comment: Copy to config/sources.json (gitignored) and fill in. Credentials come from env vars, never inlined here. Recommended GitHub auth uses auth_pools plus source/repo auth_policy. PAT pools keep failover semantics; GitHub App bot pools can route by observed rate-limit budgets. Legacy token_env/github_app/token_pools still work. graphql_url is required for GitHub/GitLab; Forgejo uses base_url and derives /api/v1. Set enabled:false to keep a source in config but skip sync runs. Optional highlight colors (hex #rgb / #rrggbb): a source-level "color", and per-repo by giving a project entry the object form {"path":..., "color":...}. A repo with no color inherits its source's; the UI can override per-repo. Most repos stay bare strings.
$comment_timezone: Optional IANA timezone the board buckets calendar days in (e.g. "Asia/Taipei"). Omit or leave empty for UTC. It aligns the UI's `today` / `this week` presets and the activity-heatmap day cells, and the range API's day boundaries, to this zone. Absolute timestamps stay UTC; only calendar-day bucketing honors it.
$comment_commit_branches: Optional per-source "commit_branches": "all" (default) also labels commits on live side branches — push events discover branches with new activity and each contributes its branch-unique commits via one compare call; "default" restricts the commit feed to the default branch only.
$comment_identities: Optional. Collapse one human's separate Repo Analytics actor identities into a single top_actors row — most useful for a GitLab person whose issues/MRs carry a username while their commits carry only an email + name. List the usernames, commit emails (matched by hash; never stored or emitted), and raw commit display names that are the same person; `name` is the canonical display. Add `source_ids` when a broad username/name/email should apply only to selected provider instances. Display-only, like colors — applied at emit time, never stored in the DB.
$comment_exclude_actors: Optional. Drop CI/dependency bots from Repo Analytics top_actors as noise (they still count in repo totals). The board already auto-drops unambiguous markers — a GitHub `[bot]` login suffix and GitLab service-account usernames (project_/group_<id>_bot_…) — so list only the unmarked ones here. Each entry matches an actor's provider username OR a display name, case-insensitively, with `*` as a wildcard.
